title: Best Times of Year to Buy Common Categories: A Seasonal Shopping Calendar
excerpt: Prices on most categories follow predictable seasonal patterns. Here's when to buy and when to wait.
author: StoreForLess Team
body:
**Electronics: after major product launches**

Prices on previous-generation electronics typically drop right after a manufacturer announces a new model, since retailers need to clear existing stock. Waiting a few weeks after a new phone, laptop, or headphone launch often means a meaningful discount on the previous version.

**Fitness equipment: January and September**

Demand spikes around New Year's resolutions and the back-to-routine period after summer, but retailers also compete hardest for this demand with discounts to capture early buyers, especially in the first two weeks of January.

**Home appliances: around major shopping holidays**

Larger kitchen appliances and home goods see their deepest discounts around major seasonal sales events, when retailers use them as headline deals to drive store traffic.

**Clothing: end of season, not during it**

Winter coats are cheapest in late winter/early spring as retailers clear inventory for the next season, not when it's actually cold. The same pattern applies in reverse for summer clothing in early fall.

**Luggage and travel gear: just after peak travel seasons**

Right after summer and major holiday travel periods, travel gear sees discounts as retailers reduce inventory before the next slower season.

We track category trends year-round on StoreForLess so you don't have to time it perfectly yourself — check current deals across every category.
